onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Securely watch a computer from a distance — or let someone watch yours. We'll set this computer up. You only need to do this once.
onboarding.roleQuestion: What will you use this computer for?
onboarding.roleHint: Pick what fits. You can choose both.
onboarding.hostTitle: I want to get help on this computer
onboarding.hostSubtitle: Someone else can then watch this screen — but only when you click “Yes” at that moment. Choose this if you want to be helped.
onboarding.clientTitle: I want to help someone else
onboarding.clientSubtitle: You watch someone else's computer to help them. Choose this if you're the one helping.
onboarding.changeLater: You can always change this later.
onboarding.serverSubtitle: A server connects you over the internet or to managed devices. You usually get its address from your administrator. You can skip this and add one later under Servers — or connect directly on your local network.
onboarding.nameHint: A name you recognise, for example “Work”.
onboarding.serverHint: The web address of the server. The app takes care of the rest.
onboarding.deviceSubtitle: This is how others see which computer is yours.

connect.noRelayBefore: No active server yet. Add one under
connect.signInSubtitle: Sign in to see the computers in your organisation.
connect.noDevices: No computers in your organisation yet.
connect.requestingSession: Requesting a session — the host may ask for approval…
connect.sessionCodeSubtitle: Ad-hoc support without an account — the host shares a code.
connect.sessionCodeNote: This connection method is still being built (server + protocol).
connect.lanSubtitle: Connect directly to another computer nearby — no server needed.
connect.lanNoneFound: No computers found yet. Make sure the other computer is sharing on this network, then search again.
connect.lanPinLabel: PIN (if the other computer shows one)
connect.lanPinHint: Leave empty to connect with this device's key instead.
connect.errUnreachable: Can't reach that computer. It's probably not sharing anymore, or it's switched off.
connect.errTimeout: The connection timed out. Is the other computer still sharing on this network?
connect.errAuth: The connection was refused. Is the code correct?
connect.errNotTrusted: This device isn't allowed yet. Enter the code, or ask the other computer to allow it under Access.

device.identitySubtitle: Give this key to a support client so they can trust it in advance (TOFU).
device.receiveHelpIntro: Let someone see this screen over the local network — no server needed. Start sharing, then read out your computer name and code.
device.waitingTitle: Waiting for a helper to connect…
device.waitingHint: Tell the helper your computer name and this code. They pick you under “Local network” and enter the code.
device.connectedHint: Someone is viewing this screen now. Stop sharing to end it.
device.pickScreenNote: When a helper connects, your system asks which screen to share.

relays.title: Saved servers
relays.name: Name
relays.serverHint: The app creates the secure connection itself.

settings.name: Name
settings.privateNote: The private key never leaves this device.

access.title: Access to this device
access.subtitle: Devices allowed to connect and view this screen without the session code.
access.removeConfirm: Remove “{name}”? It will need to be allowed again before it can connect.
access.keyHint: The other device shows this under Settings → Identity.
access.osPasswordHint: Confirms it's really you changing who can access this device.

updates.title: Updates
updates.error: Couldn't check for updates right now.

viewer.connecting: Connecting — pick a screen to share on the other computer…
